hypothesis: Systematic differences in GFN2-xTB relaxation energy (∆Erelax) between flow-matching and diffusion-based generative models reflect fundamentally different learned geometric priors—specifically, that flow-matching models generate structures with systematically larger torsion angle deviations but smaller bond length errors compared to diffusion models—and retraining flow-matching models with a torsion-angle-aware loss term derived from the GFN2-xTB energy surface will close the observed performance gap without sacrificing the sampling efficiency advantage of flow-matching approaches.
